reference.note: R (reference-vs-reference) and X (device-vs-reference) are NOT YET MEASURED. The qb2 run is genuinely computing (verified past imports + model build + CCD fetch), not estimated. Harvest via the resume path below once REF_PREDICT_DONE appears.

resume.how: Poll for REF_PREDICT_DONE in /home/ttuser/pharma_protenix_run/ref_seed{0,1}/REF_PREDICT_DONE (also echoed in ref_seed{0,1}.log). Per-seed logs: /home/ttuser/pharma_protenix_run/ref_seed{0,1}.log. Once both seeds finish, repackage each: refenv312/bin/python scripts/protenix_ref_to_harness.py /home/ttuser/pharma_protenix_run/ref_seed{N} prot -> writes structures/prot.cif + results.json. The device cif for this run was also on qb1 and is lost, so regenerate the device leg on a free card (TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=<free> tt-bio predict --model protenix-v2 -i examples/prot.yaml --seeds 0,1 ...) into dev_seed{0,1}/, then run: scripts/pharma_parity.py structures --ref-dirs ref_seed0 ref_seed1 --dev-dirs dev_seed0 dev_seed1 --label protenix-v2 --out docs/pharma-benchmark-data/protenix-v2.json
resume.additional_targets: Each reference-side seed costs several hours of CPU wall clock; run additional targets sequentially (not concurrently) to avoid CPU contention on this 32-core host.
